title: F1’s Biggest SCANDAL 😳 Crashgate Explained! #F1 #SingaporeGP #Controversy #formula1 #f1shorts
description:
🏁 Welcome to Curious F1 Girl — where we decode Formula 1 like never before.

From iconic rivalries to last-lap drama, every story in Formula One has layers… and we break them down.

The 2008 Singapore Grand Prix witnessed one of the biggest scandals in Formula One history.

Known as “Crashgate,” this controversy involved a deliberate crash that completely changed the outcome of the race.

A safety car at the perfect moment… a strategy turned upside down… and a championship ultimately decided by

title: The F1 Safety Car Driver! Bernd Maylander: 500 Races In The Safety Car | F1 Beyond The Grid
description:
At this year’s season-opening Australian Grand Prix, Bernd Maylander reached a remarkable milestone of 500 races as the FIA Safety Car Driver.

2026 F1 Japanese GP FRIDAY Practice  Analysis. 

Oscar Piastri tops Friday practice at Suzuka, leading both Mercedes drivers in a session that’s already exposing the circuit’s usual high-speed challenges. title: Verstappen And Red Bull’s Massive Problems Explained | F1 Explainer
description:
What has happened to Red Bull Racing and McLaren? The teams that once dominated Formula 1 now find themselves in deep trouble. In China, everything went wrong: Max Verstappen had issues and retired, Isack Hadjar spun, the RB22 lacks both speed and reliability, and McLaren did not even make it to the start. Meanwhile, Mercedes and Ferrari simply drove away from the field.

In this video, we analyse the major technical problems facing Red Bull and McLaren. From Max Verstappen’s disastrous weekend

title: 2026 F1 Chinese GP race analysis by Peter Windsor
description: New regs or not - McLaren-Mercedes in the race (or not): nothing could detract from the enormity of this day in Shanghai - from the first GP win for Mercedes' Kimi Antonelli. From the pole, he seized the race lead from Ferrari's Sir Lewis Hamilton on lap two - and was thereafter never headed.  Lewis re-applied pressure after the pit stops; and team-mate, George Russell, was up their in P2, pushing hard, as the race unfolded.  Kimi, though, was unperturbed.  His win was as decisive as it was auda
